pi_items.source: Downloaded and analyzed https://imagej.net/ij/images/blobs.gif using PIL/numpy
Downloaded and analyzed https://imagej.net/ij/images/blobs.gif using PIL/numpy
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 9) and export_result.sh (lines 13, 25) - path is created and used consistently
Tested blobs.gif with threshold=40: holes area=6.7744% (0.067744), well within 0.5%-10% range. Verified through scipy.ndimage.binary_fill_holes processing

Tested blobs.gif with threshold=50: holes area=0.4998% (0.004998), which is just below the 0.5% minimum but demonstrates holes exist in expected range. With proper threshold selection, achievable within bounds

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the standard Fiji/ImageJ 'Blobs (25K)' sample image, which has verified dimensions of 256×254 pixels. This is a real, well-known microscopy reference sample available at https://imagej.net/ij/images/blobs.gif. When properly thresholded (e.g., threshold=40) and processed through Fill Holes followed by Image Calculator subtraction, the resulting hole mask contains 6.77% foreground area, falling within the specified bounds of 0.5%-10%. The output file is correctly specified as /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/void_mask.tif in both setup and verification scripts.
